capacity_invariant: Default to maximum appropriate quality-preserving utilization. No quality degradation is permitted. Capacity may be reduced only by task-platform fit, quota state, resource contention, or explicit operator hold.

omitted_capability_shapes.summary: Public publication/event bus surfaces, including OMG weblog and artifact publication adapters.
Receive-only payment/support surfaces that emit governed monetary/resource receipts.
MCP/app connector tools that can provide data or mutate external systems under connector-specific authority.
Multi-agent and launcher orchestration surfaces that fan out work but are not themselves task capability units.
Sandboxed research/code-review subagents whose outputs are evidence, not autonomous dispatch capacity.
Cockpit LLM-backed commands such as research, briefing, and code-review, decomposed before routing.
The current session's inline work and self-actions, visible as a governed supply surface only through claims and receipts.
Observer/reviewer runners that can witness behavior but cannot substitute for implementation capacity.
Provider gateway model leaves that must be measured by model/profile/context/tool authority, not selected as a provider badge.
Local/burst compute families such as Ornith, Fugu, and Sakana that need measured harness/resource leaves.
Deprecated Antigrav interactive route surface retained only to refuse observed legacy deltas; Agy adapter family is distinct and route-gated.

No completion run was performed for this task; scores are asserted-not-measured until CapabilityIO or EDT witnesses outcomes.
Served-model readback must prove the actual model remains openai/gpt-5.5 and is not router, auto, or fallback substitution.

routes.launcher: scripts/hapax-agy-reviewer (review seat: blind-review prompt on stdin -> agy --print sandboxed YAML verdict on stdout)
routes.summary: Agy universal-harness review seat (Antigravity CLI; entitlement engines include Gemini, Claude, and GPT-OSS)
routes.notes: Live /usr/bin/agy CLI review harness. This is the agy reviewer family, not the retired Antigrav GUI/legacy hapax-antigrav surface and not a mutating worker lane. Gemini is an engine reachable through agy, not a capability family name. Local probe observed agy 1.0.10, eight agy models, and exact-output success with Gemini 3.1 Pro (Low); Gemini 3.5 Flash (Low) was reachable but did not obey the exact-token smoke prompt. Admission is two-stage: a fresh agy platform-capability receipt with observed CLI/wrapper evidence clears agy_review_seat_receipt_admission_required, and observed route-specific quota evidence clears route_specific_quota_receipt_absent.
known_unknowns: No durable route-specific quota or spend receipt exists yet; the route remains blocked until admission plumbing records account-live quota evidence.
Only Gemini 3.1 Pro (Low) passed an exact-output smoke prompt; Gemini 3.5 Flash (Low) was reachable but answered about CLI docs instead of the exact token.
The agy harness exposes multiple provider engines; this route is a universal-harness review seat and must not be treated as a local-private execution path.
This route is not a worker lane and does not authorize source, runtime, provider-spend, public, or governance-authority mutations.

routes.launcher: scripts/hapax-glmcp-reviewer (review seat: blind-review prompt on stdin -> YAML verdict on stdout)
routes.notes: Receipt-bounded GLM-5.2 review seat, live in cc-pr-review-dispatch as the 'glm' reviewer family. Non-worker, read-only reviewer; NOT a coding workhorse -- the GLMCP coding/dispatch promotion is separately gated by glmcp-workhorse-bakeoff-20260618 (must emit promote_to_dispatch_shadow). Admission is receipt-bounded via quota_spend_ledger RECEIPT_BOUNDED_SUBSCRIPTION_ROUTES + dispatcher_policy ROUTE_SPECIFIC_SUBSCRIPTION_QUOTA_REQUIRED. Coding Plan endpoint is primary; HAPAX_GLMCP_REVIEW_ALLOW_BASE_URL_OVERRIDE remains Coding-Plan-path-only and cannot make PAYG primary. API-credit PAYG fallback is allowed only through hapax-glmcp-reviewer, endpoint-matched admission receipts, active spend-gate evidence in the live quota ledger, and pre-call GLMCP PAYG spend receipt reservations. Set HAPAX_GLMCP_REVIEW_PAYG_FALLBACK=0 to force subscription-only behavior; HAPAX_GLMCP_REVIEW_ALLOW_PAYG_BASE_URL_OVERRIDE only changes the fallback endpoint after the same gate. Recheck with scripts/hapax-quota-telemetry-writer --json, scripts/hapax-platform-capability-freshness --route glmcp.review.direct --json, and find ~/.cache/hapax/relay/receipts -maxdepth 1 -name '*glmcp-payg-spend*.yaml' -print.
known_unknowns: Z.ai provider training/privacy posture is not equivalent to local execution; privacy/secret-sensitive review is excluded by policy.
Coding/dispatch workhorse promotion is separate and bakeoff-gated (glmcp-workhorse-bakeoff-20260618); this route is the review seat only.
Subscription quota is not exposed as account-live local telemetry; admission is receipt-bounded via the spend ledger.

When Coding Plan quota is exhausted, this review route may fall back to the official Z.ai PAYG API endpoint only with explicit API-credit receipt evidence and an active paid-spend budget gate; PAYG cannot be configured as the primary review URL.

known_unknowns: Local-inference capability scores are seed estimates anchored to the Sonnet worker baseline adjusted for a local Command-R 35B.
No local GPU quota/utilization telemetry source is wired; quota stays unknown until a local_probe-class QuotaSource lands.
Command-R 35B EXL3 context window at 24GB/32GB is unverified; max_context_class=medium is conservative.
